name: systems-programming-rust-project
description: You are a Rust project architecture expert specializing in scaffolding production-ready Rust applications. Generate complete project structures with cargo tooling, proper module organization, testing
capabilities: Generate binary, library, workspace, and web API project structures
Create Cargo.toml with proper dependencies and build profiles
Set up module organization following Rust idioms
Configure testing with unit and integration test structures
Generate CLI argument parsing with clap derive macros
Create web API scaffolding with Axum framework
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is documentation-only instructional content for Rust project scaffolding. Static analyzer flagged 41 patterns (35 external_commands, 1 network, 5 blocker) but all are false positives from markdown code blocks and template examples. No executable code or actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Building Rust applications requires proper project structure and configuration. This skill generates complete, idiomatic Rust project scaffolding with cargo tooling, module organization, testing setup, and best practices.
userTitle: Scaffold production Rust projects with cargo
limitations: Does not execute cargo commands - generates project templates only
Limited to common project types (binary, library, workspace, web API)
Does not integrate with specific databases or external services
Does not provide domain-specific business logic implementation

useCases.title: CLI Tool Development
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold a new command-line tool with argument parsing, error handling, and proper module structure using clap and anyhow.

useCases.title: Rust Library Creation
useCases.description: Generate a reusable crate with documentation examples, integration tests, and minimal dependencies for publishing to crates.io.

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building Rust web services
useCases.title: Web Service Setup
useCases.description: Create a web API project with Axum routing, middleware, database integration with SQLx, and comprehensive error handling.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Binary Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Rust binary project called 'my-cli-tool' with clap for CLI parsing and tokio for async runtime.

promptTemplates.title: Library with Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Rust library project with unit tests, integration tests, and documentation examples. Include proper error types.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a reusable crate for others to use

promptTemplates.title: Multi-crate Workspace
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a Rust workspace with three crates: core (library), api (web service), and cli (command-line interface). Share dependencies at workspace level.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a large application with multiple components

promptTemplates.title: Full Web API with Database
promptTemplates.prompt: Scaffold a production-ready web API using Axum with SQLx for PostgreSQL, including user routes, authentication middleware, and health check endpoints.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a REST API service with database backend

bestPractices: Use edition 2021 and set rust-version for compatibility guarantees
Organize code into modules by feature with clear separation of concerns
Include both unit tests in source files and integration tests in separate directory
antiPatterns: Avoid adding unnecessary dependencies - keep libraries minimal
Do not use unwrap() in production code - use proper error handling with Result
Avoid monolithic main.rs - split into logical modules early

faq.question: What Rust edition should I use?
faq.answer: Use Rust 2021 edition (current stable) with rust-version set to 1.75 or higher for modern features and long-term support.

faq.question: Should I commit Cargo.lock for libraries?
faq.answer: No, libraries should not commit Cargo.lock. Applications and binaries should commit it for reproducible builds.

faq.question: How do I add benchmarks to my project?
faq.answer: Add criterion as a dev-dependency, create a benches/ directory, and configure [[bench]] in Cargo.toml with harness = false.

faq.question: What is a Rust workspace?
faq.answer: A workspace allows multiple crates to share a single Cargo.lock and build output. Use it for multi-crate projects that depend on each other.

faq.question: How do I configure clippy for strict linting?
faq.answer: Run 'cargo clippy -- -D warnings' to deny all warnings. Create clippy.toml to customize thresholds like cognitive-complexity-threshold.

faq.question: What framework should I use for web APIs in Rust?
faq.answer: Axum is recommended for new projects - it is ergonomic, well-maintained, and built on tokio and tower. Actix-web is also popular for performance-critical applications.
